About.title: Fernanda Rocha | About
About.subtitle: Communication & Marketing professional.
About.description:
Music has always played a central role in my journey. Before becoming a profession, it was already a language, an aesthetic reference, and a way of interpreting the world. From this authentic connection comes my strategic perspective: understanding that every musical project carries identity, context, and cultural potential.

My background in design shaped how I approach positioning, image, and narrative. I see brand building, visual consistency, and creative direction as strategic tools that strengthen communication and market presence. Today, I apply this foundation to the music industry, combining artistic sensitivity with analytical thinking.

I develop music projects through an integrated approach — from building digital presence to structuring campaigns and enhancing cultural relevance. I believe music is expression, but it is also strategy. It is in balancing creative vision and performance that I build consistent and measurable results.

Jobs.title: Fernanda Padilha da Rocha | Jobs
Jobs.subtitle: A selection of campaigns handled in planning and execution — digital activation, TikTok, Instagram, and content creators.

01.title: Carry the Name — Djo
01.description: A curation-focused campaign for the track, aiming to expand the artist's relevance in the Brazilian market through cultural contextualization and digital activation.

02.title: Impose — Bad Omens
02.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with activation directed at TikTok and encouraging the use of the audio within the platform's culture.

03.title: Desabafo de Um Jovem Adulto — Grelo
03.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, directed at activation on TikTok and Instagram, encouraging the creation of content aligned with the song's narrative.

04.title: Subconsciente — Lou Garcia
04.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with TikTok activation and expanded reach through creators and content aligned with the artist's positioning.

05.title: Don't Speak French — Rose Gray
05.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with creative direction toward GRWM-format content on TikTok, aiming for organic activation and audience connection.

06.title: Au Pays du Cocaine — Geese
06.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, directed at trend activation on TikTok, driving massive audio usage and organic growth on the platform.

07.title: Festa do Tubarão (55X Remix) — 55X
07.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with trend activation on TikTok, prioritizing impact and engagement within the platform's culture.

08.title: O Que Eu Faço Agora? — Dilsinho
08.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with activation on Instagram and TikTok, involving creators and pages to expand reach and generate organic volume.

09.title: Saudade do Carai — Mari Fernandez
09.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with activation directed at TikTok and encouraging massive audio usage on the platform.

10.title: A Paz — Maneva
10.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with activation on TikTok and Instagram, connecting creators and pages to expand reach and strengthen the project's digital presence.

11.title: Estranhos de Novo — Felipe Araujo
11.description: Strategic campaign focused on the track, with activation on TikTok and Instagram, connecting creators and pages to expand reach and encourage organic audio usage.

12.title: Antes Que Você Vá — Giulia Be
12.description: Strategic campaign focused on TikTok, with activation directed at edit pages and creators, encouraging the creation of emotional content and expanding the track's organic reach.

13.title: Minha Gata — Dilsinho
13.description: Strategic campaign focused on TikTok, with activation directed at edit pages and creators, encouraging content creation and expanding organic audio usage.

14.title: Work — No Na
14.description: Strategic campaign focused on TikTok, with activation aimed at choreography content and dance creators, encouraging audio repetition and expanding the track's organic reach.

PrivacyPolicy.title: Fernanda Padilha da Rocha | Privacy Policy
PrivacyPolicy.subtitle: How we collect, use, and protect your information.

01.title: Information We Collect
01.content: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name and email address when reaching out through this site's contact form. We may also collect anonymous browsing data — such as pages visited and time spent — through analytics tools.

02.title: How We Use Your Information
02.content: The information collected is used solely to respond to your messages, improve the browsing experience on this site, and occasionally send relevant communications — always with your prior consent.

03.title: Data Sharing
03.content: We do not sell, trade, or transfer your personal information to third parties. Your data may only be shared with service providers who assist in operating this site, provided they agree to keep it confidential.

04.title: Cookies
04.content: This site may use cookies to enhance the user experience. You may choose to disable cookies in your browser settings, though this may affect some site functionality.

05.title: Your Rights
05.content: You have the right to access, correct, or request the deletion of your personal data at any time. To exercise these rights, please reach out through the contact form available on this site.

06.title: Changes to This Policy
06.content: This privacy policy may be updated periodically. We recommend reviewing it regularly. The date of the last update will always be indicated at the top of this page.

07.title: Contact
07.content: If you have any questions about this privacy policy or how your data is handled, please get in touch through the contact form available on this site.
